title: Add Gemini vision backend
description: Implement internal/vision/gemini/ adapter using the Google Gemini API (base64 image input). Wire up via VISION_BACKEND=gemini in config/main.go alongside existing claude/ollama backends. Needed to enable multi-model benchmark comparison.

title: Upload: analyzing banner shows but photo not visible until refresh
description: When uploading a photo for an area, the analyzing banner appears but no photo is shown behind it during the streaming upload. If you refresh the page, the photo appears correctly with the analyzing banner overlaid on top as expected. The photo should be visible behind the analyzing banner during the upload stream, not just after a page refresh.

title: Improve item editing UX: click any cell to edit, number picker for qty
description: Currently clicking on an item row only triggers edit if the user clicks the name cell. Clicking qty, location, or other cells does nothing. All cells should be clickable to start editing. Additionally, the inline qty edit input should be type=number (a number picker/spinner) on all platforms including mobile, but currently it renders as a plain text input on mobile.
close_reason: PR #35 merged: click any cell to edit, focus follows clicked cell

title: Publish a CI container image
description: Each CI job currently installs tools from scratch on every run (golangci-lint, staticcheck, govulncheck, Go, Node, Playwright + Chromium). This adds minutes of setup time and risks flaky installs from upstream sources. Build and publish a Docker image that has all CI dependencies pre-installed, then update .github/workflows/ci.yml to use it as the job container. The image should include: Go (pinned to go.mod version), golangci-lint v2.10.1, staticcheck, govulncheck, Node 22, Playwright Chromium with system deps. Consider publishing to ghcr.io (GitHub Container Registry) using a separate workflow that rebuilds on changes to the Dockerfile or a lockfile of tool versions. The image should be versioned/tagged so CI jobs pin to a specific digest rather than latest.

title: New area dialog: error messages are hidden behind blurred background
description: When creating a new area via the dialog, any error messages (e.g. duplicate name, validation errors) are not visible to the user because they appear behind the dialog's blurred background overlay. Errors should be shown inline within the dialog itself so the user can see and act on them.
notes: Reopened: the fix switched to toasts but toasts are rendered behind the dialog backdrop, so they're still not visible when the dialog is open. Need to either show the error inline inside the dialog, or ensure toasts appear above the dialog (z-index). Reproduced: trigger a duplicate name error while the create area dialog is open.
close_reason: Fixed: inline error in dialog; PR #24 merged

title: Show full photo without cropping in area card
description: The area photo is currently displayed with object-fit: cover which crops the image to fill the container. Instead, the full photo should be visible (object-fit: contain or similar) so no part of the image is cut off.
close_reason: Fixed: object-fit contain, no cropping; PR #30

title: Final quality gate: make all green
description: Run 'make all' which executes lint + vet + staticcheck + test + build. Fix any issues found by golangci-lint, go vet, or staticcheck. Ensure zero lint errors and all tests pass with -race flag. Verify 'make docker-build' succeeds. This is the final sign-off step before the project is ready for use.

title: Dockerfile and docker-compose
description: Create multi-stage Dockerfile: builder stage uses golang:1.26-alpine with CGO_ENABLED=0 (works because modernc.org/sqlite is pure Go), runtime stage uses alpine:3.21 with ca-certificates and tzdata. Create docker-compose.yml with kitchinv service (port 8080, kitchinv-data volume at /data) and ollama sidecar service (ollama/ollama:latest, ollama-models volume). Add make docker-build, make docker-up, make docker-pull-model targets to Makefile.

title: Areas: hide item table and add-item row while analysis is in progress
description: While an area photo is being analyzed, the item table and the 'add item' row should not be shown. Only the analyzing banner/overlay should be visible. Once analysis completes, the table and add-item controls should appear with the results.
close_reason: Fixed: items section hidden during analysis; PR #29

title: Allow user to edit inventory table
description: Add inline or modal editing capability to the inventory table so users can update item fields (name, quantity, unit, etc.) directly from the table view.

title: Design frontend status indicator
description: Design the UI for the vision system status indicator in the banner. Should show connected/disconnected/checking states with appropriate color and iconography consistent with the Cold Storage dark theme. No code yet — just the design spec.

title: Allow re-analyzing the same photo without re-uploading
description: Add a Re-analyse button on the area detail page that triggers analysis on the already-uploaded photo without requiring the user to select the file again. Needs a new endpoint or parameter to trigger analysis on the stored photo.
close_reason: No longer needed after frontend redesign — users can simply re-upload the photo

title: Implement versioning, changelog, and semver tagging in CI
description: Research and implement a versioning strategy for kitchinv. Goals: (1) conventional commits enforced (commitizen or commitlint), (2) automatic changelog generation from commit history, (3) automatic semver tagging on merge to main via CI. Evaluate options: commitizen, semantic-release, release-please, or a lightweight Go-native approach. Must integrate with the existing GitHub Actions CI pipeline.
notes:
Options to evaluate:

- release-please (Google): GitHub Action that reads conventional commits, bumps semver, generates CHANGELOG.md, and opens a release PR. Very low friction, no local tooling required. Best fit for this repo given the PR approval workflow — the release PR acts as a natural gate.
- semantic-release: Fully automated, runs in CI, handles tagging + GitHub release + changelog. More config but more powerful. Skips the release PR step so less aligned with the manual approval process.
- commitizen: Enforces conventional commit format at commit time via an interactive prompt. Usually paired with one of the above for the release side. Could replace free-form commit messages.
- git-cliff: Lightweight Rust-based changelog generator. Easy to drop into a Makefile or CI step. Good option if we want changelog generation without the full release automation.

Recommendation: release-please for release automation + commitizen for enforcing conventional commits at write time.

title: Config package
description: Implement internal/config/config.go with Load() that reads env vars and applies defaults. Write table-driven config_test.go using t.Setenv. Env vars: LISTEN_ADDR, DB_PATH, VISION_BACKEND, OLLAMA_HOST, OLLAMA_MODEL, CLAUDE_API_KEY, CLAUDE_MODEL, PHOTO_BACKEND, PHOTO_LOCAL_PATH.

title: Headed Playwright over SSH for visual debugging
description:
When debugging E2E visual issues (e.g. spinner appears in wrong place), running Playwright in headed mode would help. Problem: session is SSH with no display.

Options to investigate:
- Xvfb virtual framebuffer + x11vnc to expose display over VNC
- noVNC (browser-based VNC client) so display is accessible via browser without a VNC client
- Playwright's built-in --headed with DISPLAY=:99 set to Xvfb

Goal: run 'npx playwright test --headed' from the SSH worktree and be able to observe the browser visually.

Working directory: /home/vbonduro/git/github.com/vbonduro/kitchinv/.claude/worktrees/velvet-enchanting-rainbow

title: Compress images client-side before upload if they exceed the 5 MB limit
description: The Claude vision API rejects images larger than 5 MB (base64-encoded). Currently, users who upload a large photo (e.g. a high-res phone photo) get a silent failure or an error toast with no guidance. We should compress/resize the image client-side in the browser before sending it, so that uploads reliably succeed regardless of input size. Considerations: - Use the Canvas API to resize/re-encode the image to JPEG before POSTing - Choose a target size / quality that keeps the base64-encoded result under 5 MB - Preserve aspect ratio - Should be transparent to the user (no extra step required) - May want to show a brief 'Optimising...' indicator if compression takes noticeable time

title: Area list not updated dynamically when adding a new area
description: When creating a new area via the dropdown form, the HTMX response returns an empty body because renderPartial executes tmpl.Templates()[0] which is the unnamed root template, not the named 'area_card' define block. The new area card is never inserted into .area-list, so the user sees nothing until they refresh. Fix renderPartial to execute the named template by name, or restructure the partial to not use a define block.
notes:
## Investigation & Work Done (2026-02-22 session)

### Root cause confirmed
HTMX 1.9 silently drops the POST /areas request when hx-target='#area-list' is not found in the DOM (e.g. when navigating from area detail page). Fix: replaced HTMX form with plain fetch()-based addArea() JS in base.html that handles both cases — on /areas page appends new card, otherwise navigates to /areas.

### Fix implemented (base.html)
The addArea() JS function is already merged to main as part of a broader PR. The two new E2E tests for this bug are on branch fix-area-list-dynamic-update.

### E2E test isolation work (blocking progress)
Tried to add beforeEach resetDB() for clean state between tests. Discovered deep isolation issues:

**Root cause of isolation failure**: One server process, one SQLite DB, shared across all tests. resetDB() calls DELETE FROM areas + DELETE FROM sqlite_sequence but this is fragile because:
1. sqlite_sequence only resets correctly if no other test has left rows between resets
2. Other spec files (areas, upload, navigation, regression) all run before search.spec.ts
3. The cumulative state from prior suites causes area IDs to be non-1 when search tests run

**What was tried**:
- POST /control/reset endpoint (DELETE FROM areas + sqlite_sequence reset) — works in isolation but fragile across suites
- Cache-Control: no-store on search HTMX responses — doesn't fix isolation
- hx-history=false on #search-results — doesn't fix isolation  
- browser.newContext() in beforeEach — doesn't fix root DB issue
- In-memory SQLite (db.OpenForTesting() in test mode) — still same server, same shared DB

**Key insight from Playwright docs (test-parallel)**:
The correct pattern is worker-scoped fixtures where each worker owns its own server instance. With workers: 1 all tests share one worker/server. The fix is to either:

### Recommended path forward
Use Playwright workerIndex to give each worker its own server on a distinct port. Each worker starts fresh in-memory DB, no shared state, no resets needed. With workers > 1 this also enables parallel test execution.

Implementation sketch:
- global-setup: don't start the server (or start mock-ollama only)
- Add a worker-scoped fixture in e2e/fixtures.ts that starts kitchinv on port (9090 + workerIndex), uses in-memory DB (KITCHINV_TEST_MODE=1), and tears it down after the worker finishes
- Update playwright.config.ts baseURL to use the worker's port (via fixture)
- Remove all resetDB() calls — not needed with per-worker server

### Current branch state
Branch: fix-area-list-dynamic-update (worktree: moonlit-imagining-blossom)
Files changed:
- internal/config/config.go — TestMode bool
- internal/db/db.go — Reset() + sqlite_sequence reset; OpenForTesting() already existed
- internal/web/server.go — EnableTestMode(), handleTestReset (POST /control/reset)
- cmd/kitchinv/main.go — uses db.OpenForTesting() when TestMode, needs sql import
- e2e/global-setup.ts — KITCHINV_TEST_MODE=1
- e2e/fixtures.ts — resetDB() helper (may be removable with per-worker approach)
- e2e/playwright.config.ts — workers=1, mobile-chrome depends on chromium
- e2e/tests/areas.spec.ts — beforeEach resetDB(), two new kitchinv-49a bug tests
- e2e/tests/upload.spec.ts — beforeEach resetDB()
- e2e/tests/search.spec.ts — setupAreaWithItems() on same page fixture, beforeEach resetDB()
- e2e/tests/navigation.spec.ts — beforeAll resetDB()
- e2e/tests/regression.spec.ts — beforeAll resetDB()
- internal/web/templates/base.html — addArea() JS replacing HTMX form
- internal/web/templates/pages/search.html — hx-history=false (can revert)
- internal/web/handler_search.go — Cache-Control: no-store (can revert)
close_reason: Completed and merged in feat-frontend-rework PR

title: Stream vision analysis results to browser in real-time
description: Replace the current fire-and-wait approach with streaming. Switch the Ollama adapter from stream:false to stream:true, parse tokens incrementally as they arrive, and push results to the browser via SSE or chunked transfer. Items should fade in one-by-one as they are parsed from the stream. Covers: (1) streaming Ollama adapter, (2) SSE or chunked HTTP handler, (3) frontend JS to consume the stream and animate items fading in as each line completes.

title: Remove location text field in favour of bounding box coordinates
description:
The current vision prompt asks the model to return a text 'location' field per item (e.g. 'top shelf, left side'). Now that bounding box coordinates are stored and displayed visually (kitchinv-dht), the text location field is redundant and should be removed.

## Work required
- Remove location from the vision prompt
- Remove location from the vision response JSON schema and ParseJSONResponse
- Remove location column from the DB schema (migration)
- Remove location from the detected item struct and any API responses
- Remove location from any UI table columns or item displays
- Update tests

title: Photo preview missing and analysis state lost on navigation
description:
Two related issues on the area detail page:

1. Photo preview: When the user selects a file and hits Analyse, the photo should appear in the photo block immediately. Currently createObjectURL is called after form submit but the image does not render reliably. The photo should appear as soon as the file is selected (onchange), not onsubmit.

2. Analysis state not persisted across navigation: The streaming analysis runs entirely in frontend JS (fetch + ReadableStream). If the user navigates away mid-analysis and returns, the stream is dead and the page shows no indication that analysis was/is in progress. The server continues writing items to the DB, but the frontend loses all state.

Fix plan:
- Move photo preview to the file input onchange handler so it shows immediately on selection, before the user even clicks Analyse.
- For navigation resilience: on page load, check if there is a photo but zero items for this area (a signal that analysis may be in progress or recently completed). If so, poll or re-fetch the item list after a short delay. Alternatively, persist the in-progress state in sessionStorage keyed by areaID and resume showing the spinner + item list on re-load until items are confirmed present.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #9: photo preview onchange, mobile file picker (CSS display:none, capture attr), context cancellation on navigation, sessionStorage replaced with server-driven polling, areas list analysing state

title: Show timestamp for when area image was uploaded
description: On the area card (or area detail view), display a human-readable timestamp showing when the current photo was uploaded. This helps users know how recently the inventory was scanned.

title: Structured JSON prompt with error codes for vision response
description:
## Problem

The current vision prompt asks for plain-text pipe-delimited output (name | quantity | notes). This is fragile:
- The model can drift from the format (prefixes like 'Here are the items:', numbered lists, etc.)
- There is no way to distinguish 'no food found' from a parse failure
- Partial failures (one item the model isn't sure about) are silently dropped or garbled
- parse.go has ad-hoc heuristics to skip header lines

## Goal

Change AnalysisPrompt (internal/vision/vision.go) to request a JSON response with explicit status codes so parse errors and 'no items found' cases are surfaced cleanly.

## Proposed response schema

```json
{
  "status": "ok" | "no_items" | "not_food" | "unclear",
  "items": [
    {
      "name": "string",
      "quantity": "string or null",
      "notes": "string or null"
    }
  ]
}
```

Status codes:
- ok        – one or more items found; items array is populated
- no_items  – image is a valid food storage area but nothing is visible/identifiable
- not_food  – image does not appear to be a food storage area
- unclear   – image is too blurry, dark, or otherwise unreadable

## Implementation plan

1. Update AnalysisPrompt in vision.go to request the JSON schema above
2. Replace ParseResponse and ParseLine in parse.go with JSON-aware equivalents:
   - ParseJSONResponse(raw string) ([]DetectedItem, string, error) returning items + status + parse error
   - ParseJSONLine is not needed for the batch path; streaming still needs line-by-line accumulation (see below)
3. Streaming consideration: the model will emit the JSON incrementally. Options:
   a. Buffer the entire JSON response before emitting items (simplest, slight latency increase)
   b. Stream individual item objects as soon as each complete array element closes (complex but fast)
   Start with (a) — buffer full JSON, then parse and emit all items at once. Add a TODO for (b).
4. Update AnalysisResult to include Status string field
5. Update Analyze() callers (handler_upload.go / area_service.go) to check status and log/skip on non-ok
6. Update all vision adapter unit tests and service tests

## Acceptance criteria
- A 'not_food' or 'no_items' response results in zero items written to DB and a clear log message, not a parse error
- An unparseable response is surfaced as an error, not silently swallowed
- All existing unit tests pass with updated stubs
- parse_test.go covers ok, no_items, not_food, unclear, and malformed JSON cases

title: Automate dev dependency installation
description: Dev setup currently requires manually installing several tools (Go, Node, golangci-lint, staticcheck, govulncheck, direnv, gitleaks) following README instructions. Find and implement a convenient way to automate this — e.g. a bootstrap script, Makefile target, or tool like mise/asdf — so new contributors and CI can get a working dev environment with a single command. Consider: pinning tool versions, cross-platform support (Linux/macOS), and not conflicting with existing PATH-based tool discovery in the Makefile.

title: Make CI checks runnable locally
description:
## Problem

'make test' (and other make targets) fail locally because the Makefile uses bare 'go'/'golangci-lint' commands but these tools are not on the system PATH. Go lives at ~/.local/bin/go/bin/go and golangci-lint is installed ad-hoc (e.g. /tmp/golangci-bin/golangci-lint). This means developers must remember magic paths to run any check, and there is no parity with CI.

## Goals

1. Fix Makefile so that 'make test', 'make lint', 'make vet', 'make all', etc. all work out of the box locally without remembering non-standard paths.
2. Provide a way to run the full CI suite locally, ideally in the same environment as GitHub Actions (e.g. via 'act' or a documented Docker-based approach).
3. The fix should be simple and not require every developer to edit their shell profile.

## Acceptance criteria

- 'make test' passes on a fresh shell (no PATH modifications needed)
- 'make lint' runs golangci-lint without a manual path prefix
- A single command (e.g. 'make ci' or 'act') runs all four CI jobs (lint, vet, staticcheck, test) in an environment close to ubuntu-latest
- README or CLAUDE.md documents how to run CI locally

## Possible approaches

- Detect go binary location in Makefile (GO ?= $(shell which go || echo ~/.local/bin/go/bin/go))
- Add a 'make ci' target that shells into the project's Docker image or uses 'act' to replay .github/workflows/ci.yml
- Document 'act' setup (nektos/act) as the canonical local CI runner

title: Ollama vision adapter
description: Implement internal/vision/ollama/ollama.go as OllamaAnalyzer implementing VisionAnalyzer. Uses github.com/ollama/ollama/api client. Prompt: 'List every food item you can see in this refrigerator/freezer/pantry photo. For each item provide: name, approximate quantity, and any relevant notes (e.g. opened, expired). Respond in plain text, one item per line, format: name | quantity | notes'. Write ollama_test.go using httptest.NewServer with canned Ollama JSON responses.

title: Project scaffold and tooling setup
description: Initialize the Go module, create the minimal cmd/kitchinv/main.go stub, Makefile, .gitignore, and .golangci.yml. Verify 'make build' produces a binary.

title: Create UI mockup screenshots
description: Create ASCII/text mockups embedded in the README (or as separate docs/screenshots/) showing: the areas list page, the area detail page (with photo + item list), the upload flow, and the search page. These should be realistic representations of the Pico.css + HTMX UI.

title: Run benchmark across models and iterate on prompts
description: Using reference fixture photos + ground truth provided by @vbonduro, run the benchmark runner across Claude Sonnet, Claude Opus, and Gemini. Analyze gaps in item identification and quantity accuracy. Iterate on ClaudeSystemPrompt, ClaudeUserPrompt in internal/vision/vision.go based on findings. Commit final prompts + benchmark result artifacts.
close_reason: Completed: benchmarked gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-3-flash-preview, claude-haiku/sonnet/opus across 6 fixtures. gemini-2.5-flash recommended as production backend at ~54% adjusted accuracy. Full results in docs/vision-model-benchmark.md.

title: Document Unraid deployment in README
description: Add an Unraid deployment section to the README covering: (1) Tailscale prerequisite — install Tailscale plugin on Unraid, add kitchinv to tailnet only, do not expose port 8080 to the internet; (2) Key file setup — create /mnt/user/appdata/kitchinv/secrets/gemini_key with chmod 600, explain why key files are used instead of env vars; (3) Container install via Community Apps template; (4) Updating — docker pull + container restart when new version tags are published to GHCR.

title: Rename area: generic error shown when name already exists
description: When renaming an area to a name that is already used by another area, the error message shown is the generic 'failed to rename area'. It should instead show a clear message indicating that another area already has that name, e.g. 'An area with this name already exists'.

title: Service layer: AreaService
description: Implement internal/service/area_service.go with AreaService struct. Define AreaStorer, ItemStorer, PhotoStorer interfaces within the service package. Key method: ProcessPhoto(ctx, areaID, r, mimeType) which: 1) saves raw bytes to photoStore, 2) inserts photo row, 3) runs vision analysis, 4) calls ReplaceForArea to replace all items for the area. Write area_service_test.go using hand-written testify/mock mocks for all interfaces.

title: Upload: page does not auto-update after analysis completes
description: After a photo is uploaded and analysis finishes streaming, the page does not automatically update to show the inventory items. The user has to manually refresh the page to see the results. The SSE stream should trigger a UI update when analysis completes.
close_reason: Superseded by kitchinv-ehi: streaming will be removed in favour of a single update after analysis completes

title: Fix CSP: move inline scripts to external files with hashes
description: Currently all JavaScript is inline (onclick attributes, inline <script> blocks), which requires 'unsafe-inline' in script-src to work. The proper fix is to move all JS into a separate static .js file served by the app, and either remove the hash requirement (since script-src 'self' covers same-origin files) or add per-script hashes/nonces. This removes the need for 'unsafe-inline' and restores the XSS protection that CSP is meant to provide.

title: Architecture and design review (Ousterhout persona)
description:
## Goal

Run a two-agent design review loop over the kitchinv codebase.

## Process

Spawn two subagents in sequence:

### Agent 1 — Reviewer (John Ousterhout persona)
Adopt the persona of John Ousterhout, author of *A Philosophy of Software Design*. Apply his specific heuristics when reviewing:
- Deep modules vs shallow modules (large interface hiding complexity is good; tiny classes/functions that expose their internals are bad)
- Information hiding — are implementation details leaking through interfaces?
- Define errors out of existence — are errors surfaced that callers can't reasonably handle?
- Strategic vs tactical programming — are there signs of tactical complexity (workarounds, special cases, ad-hoc parsing) that a strategic design would eliminate?
- Comments should describe *why*, not *what* — are there missing or low-value comments?
- Complexity creep — is the overall system complexity proportional to the problem it solves?

The reviewer reads all relevant source files, produces a structured written review with:
1. A brief overall assessment
2. A prioritised list of findings (each with: location, Ousterhout principle violated, proposed fix)
3. A list of things done well

The reviewer writes findings to a temporary review artifact (e.g. a scratch file or structured output returned to the orchestrator).

### Agent 2 — Developer
Receives the reviewer's findings and implements fixes for each actionable item. For each finding:
- Decides whether to fix or defer (with rationale)
- Implements the fix on the current branch
- Notes what was changed

## Scope
Review all non-generated Go source under internal/ and cmd/, plus the frontend template. Exclude vendor/, generated files, and test files from the primary review (test files may be noted but are lower priority).

## Acceptance criteria
- All P0/P1 findings from the review are either fixed or have a documented deferral rationale
- make all passes after developer agent completes
- A summary of review findings and resolutions is captured in the bead notes
notes:
## Ousterhout Review Findings

### P0 — Critical

**P0-1: newVisionAnalyzer returns nil on misconfiguration**
- Location: cmd/kitchinv/main.go
- Principle: Define errors out of existence
- Fix: Change return type to (vision.VisionAnalyzer, error), fatal exit in main on error
- Status: NOT YET IMPLEMENTED

**P0-2: AreaService depends on concrete store types, not interfaces**
- Location: internal/service/area_service.go
- Principle: Deep modules; information hiding
- Fix: Define private areaRepository, photoRepository, itemRepository interfaces in service package; change struct fields to use those interfaces; remove store import
- Status: NOT YET IMPLEMENTED

### P1 — Important

**P1-1: StreamAnalyzer stale comment references non-existent *StreamError type**
- Location: internal/vision/vision.go, StreamAnalyzer interface
- Fix: Update AnalyzeStream doc comment to describe StreamEvent.Err contract accurately
- Status: NOT YET IMPLEMENTED

**P1-2: Duplicated HTTP request construction in Claude adapter**
- Location: internal/vision/claude/claude.go
- Fix: Extract buildMessages() and newHTTPRequest() helpers; add anthropicVersion constant; use in both Analyze and AnalyzeStream
- Status: NOT YET IMPLEMENTED

**P1-3: Ad-hoc prefix filtering in ParseLine (open-ended heuristic list)**
- Location: internal/vision/parse.go
- Fix: Replace HasPrefix block with structural check: require '|' in line to be considered an item
- Status: NOT YET IMPLEMENTED

**P1-4: web.Server depends on concrete *service.AreaService**
- Location: internal/web/server.go
- Fix: Define kitchenService interface in web package; change Server.service field and NewServer param to use it
- Status: NOT YET IMPLEMENTED

**P1-5: config.Load conflates loading and validation, never returns error**
- Location: internal/config/config.go
- Fix: Return (*Config, error); validate CLAUDE_API_KEY when VISION_BACKEND=claude at load time. Resolves root cause of P0-1.
- Status: NOT YET IMPLEMENTED (deferred — P0-1 fix addresses the symptom sufficiently)

### P2 — Minor

**P2-1: Down migration files embedded/parsed but never executed (dead code)**
- Location: internal/db/db.go
- Fix: Add comment 'Only up migrations applied; down migrations not executed. Rollback not supported.'
- Status: DEFERRED — removing down files would restructure migrations directory convention

**P2-2: logging.New sets global slog default as side effect**
- Location: internal/logging/logging.go
- Fix: Remove slog.SetDefault() from New(); call it explicitly in main after logger creation; update comment
- Status: NOT YET IMPLEMENTED

**P2-3: PhotoStore name collides between photostore pkg (interface) and store pkg (concrete type)**
- Location: internal/photostore/photostore.go vs internal/store/photo_store.go
- Fix: Rename photostore.PhotoStore interface to PhotoStorage or PhotoBackend
- Status: DEFERRED — cosmetic, touches many callsites

**P2-4: Missing 'why' comments on non-obvious decisions**
- Locations:
  - internal/db/db.go: explain cache=shared, WAL, foreign_keys DSN options
  - internal/vision/claude/claude.go: explain MaxTokens: 1024 magic number
  - internal/vision/claude/claude.go normaliseMIME: explain jpeg fallback rationale
  - internal/vision/claude/claude.go + ollama/ollama.go: explain channel buffer size 16
- Status: NOT YET IMPLEMENTED

### Things Done Well (from review)
- VisionAnalyzer interface is appropriately deep
- domain/types.go is pure and import-free
- config.Load is flat and readable
- ParseResponse/ParseLine are correctly separated
- photostore.PhotoStore interface is well-scoped
- db.OpenForTesting is a first-class citizen
- web handlers are thin and delegate to service
close_reason: Ousterhout design review complete. All P0/P1 findings implemented, P2s addressed or deferred with rationale. PR #13 merged.

title: Extract bounding box coordinates for detected items from vision LLM
description:
When analysing a kitchen photo, ask the vision model to return bounding box coordinates (x, y, width, height or normalized x1,y1,x2,y2) for each detected item alongside the name and quantity. Store the bounding box data in the DB alongside the item record so it can be used for UI highlighting later.

## Work required
- Update the vision prompt to request bounding box coordinates per item
- Update the vision response JSON schema and ParseJSONResponse to include bbox fields
- Update the DB schema to store bbox per detected item (x, y, w, h as floats, nullable)
- Update the item ingestion path to persist bbox data
- Verify gemini-2.5-flash returns usable coordinates (test against benchmark fixtures)

title: Security vulnerability analysis
description: Analyze the kitchinv codebase for security vulnerabilities. Areas to cover: (1) dependency audit (govulncheck, nancy, or similar), (2) OWASP top 10 review of HTTP handlers — path traversal, injection, auth, CSRF, (3) file upload handling safety, (4) SQLite query parameterization, (5) environment variable / secret handling (API keys), (6) Docker image security (base image, non-root user, attack surface). Produce a prioritized list of findings and recommended fixes.

title: Add feedback button in header that files GitHub issues
description: Add a feedback button in the page header that opens a new GitHub issue on the kitchinv repository. Clicking the button should open the GitHub new issue page (https://github.com/vbonduro/kitchinv/issues/new) in a new tab, allowing users to report bugs or request features directly from the app.

title: PhotoStore interface and local filesystem adapter
description: Create internal/photostore/photostore.go with PhotoStore interface (Save, Get, Delete methods). Implement internal/photostore/local/local.go as LocalPhotoStore storing files at basePath/prefix/<uuid>.<ext>. Write local_test.go using t.TempDir() as base path, testing round-trip Save/Get/Delete.

title: Write README.md
description: Create a comprehensive README.md for the kitchinv project. Should cover: what the project is and why it exists, architecture overview, environment variable config table, deployment with docker-compose (including pulling the Ollama model), switching to Claude backend, local development setup, make targets, and project directory structure.

title: Audit E2E tests: replace time-based waits with state-based assertions
description: Several E2E tests use hardcoded timeouts (waitForTimeout) to wait for async operations to complete. Time-based waits are inherently flaky on slow CI runners. Audit all E2E test files and replace waitForTimeout calls with state-based assertions (waitFor, expect(...).toBeVisible, toHaveCount, etc.) wherever possible. Where a wait is genuinely unavoidable (e.g. WithoutCancel goroutine drain), document why and keep it minimal.
close_reason: Per-test server isolation eliminates all shared state; time-based waits in navigation tests are now inherent to the feature (context.WithoutCancel delay), not brittleness

title: Add govulncheck to CI pipeline
description: Add govulncheck as a CI job in .github/workflows/ci.yml to automatically scan for known Go vulnerabilities on every push/PR. This ensures dependency vulnerabilities are caught early.

title: Define deployment target and strategy
description:
Deploy kitchinv as a Docker container on Unraid, accessible only via Tailscale VPN. Security model: no internet exposure (Tailscale as the perimeter), API keys stored in chmod 600 files in appdata (not env vars, to avoid leakage via process listings and docker inspect).

## Deployment target
- Unraid server running the kitchinv Docker container
- Image pulled from GHCR on version tags (publish workflow already in place)
- Unraid Community Apps template for easy install/update

## Security approach
- **Network**: kitchinv not exposed to the internet. Access via Tailscale only (Tailscale Unraid plugin).
- **Keys**: GEMINI_API_KEY_FILE / CLAUDE_API_KEY_FILE pointing to chmod 600 files in /mnt/user/appdata/kitchinv/secrets/. Keys never appear in process listings, docker inspect, or env output.
- Accept that physical drive access = key access (array is not encrypted).

## Sub-issues
1. Add *_KEY_FILE support to config.go
2. Write Unraid Community Apps template
3. Document deployment in README (Tailscale setup, key file setup, container install)
4. GitHub Actions: document manual docker pull update flow (no auto-deploy for home server)

title: Database layer: migrations and db.go
description: Write the 3 SQL migration pairs in internal/db/migrations/ (areas, photos, items tables). Implement internal/db/db.go: open modernc.org/sqlite, enable WAL mode and foreign keys via DSN pragmas, run migrations via golang-migrate with iofs source. Write db_test.go using :memory: SQLite to verify migrations apply and roll back.

title: Design health-check API endpoint
description: Design the /health or /api/status endpoint that checks whether the configured vision backend is reachable. Define the response shape (JSON), what constitutes 'healthy' for Ollama vs Claude backends, and any timeout/error handling considerations. No code yet — just the design spec.

title: Implement vision status monitor
description: Implement the health-check API endpoint and frontend status indicator per the designs in kitchinv-36v and kitchinv-b9d. Includes: Go handler for the status endpoint, VisionAnalyzer health-check method or equivalent, HTMX polling in the banner, and CSS for the indicator states.

title: Add structured logging with log file support
description: Currently the server only logs to stdout with log.Printf. There is no log file, no request tracing, and no way to diagnose issues after the fact. Add structured logging (e.g. log/slog) with: (1) configurable log level via env var, (2) log file output in addition to stderr, (3) per-request trace logging (method, path, status, duration), (4) enough context in error logs to diagnose failures without needing a debugger.

title: Upload: area card not interactive until page refresh
description: After uploading a photo and analysis completes, the area card's item list, edit, and delete controls are unresponsive. The user must manually refresh the page before they can expand the item list or interact with any item controls. The HTMX/JS event bindings on the dynamically updated card are likely not being re-attached after the upload response updates the DOM.

title: Architect grocery list feature
description:
Design the grocery list feature end-to-end. Key questions to resolve:

1. **How do we determine what to buy?**
   - User manually adds items to the list
   - Derive from inventory (items with low/zero quantity)
   - AI-suggested based on photo analysis (e.g. 'running low on X')
   - Combination of the above

2. **Where can we export the list?**
   - Copy to clipboard (plain text)
   - Share via native share API (mobile)
   - Export as CSV/PDF
   - Integration with external apps (e.g. Apple Reminders, Google Tasks, AnyList)
   - Print view

3. **How will we manage the list?**
   - Persistent (saved to DB) vs ephemeral (session only)
   - Check off items while shopping
   - Group by area/category
   - Quantity tracking
   - Multiple lists (e.g. weekly shop vs party supplies)

4. **Where does it live in the UI?**
   - New nav section
   - Sidebar/drawer
   - Per-area 'add to list' button

This bead is for architecture/design only — no implementation yet.

title: Clean up README.md
description: The README.md needs cleanup: remove the ASCII screenshots and add badges for Go Report Card and code coverage.

title: Display item bounding box highlight on area photo in search results and hover
description:
Using bounding box coordinates stored in the DB (see kitchinv-8r0), highlight the corresponding region of the kitchen photo when a user hovers over an item or it appears in search results.

## UX behaviour
- In search results: when an item card is hovered/focused, overlay the bounding box on the area photo with a highlight rectangle
- On area detail view: hovering an item in the list highlights its region on the photo
- Highlight should be a semi-transparent coloured rectangle or outline drawn over the image

## Work required
- Add bbox coordinates to the item API response
- Implement SVG or canvas overlay on the area photo that renders a rectangle given normalised coordinates
- Wire hover/focus events on item elements to show/hide the overlay
- Handle cases where bbox is null (items detected before this feature was shipped)

title: Add end-to-end automated tests
description:
Write E2E tests that drive a real browser against a running kitchinv instance. Evaluate Playwright or chromedp. Cover key user flows: add an area, upload a photo, verify items appear, search for an item, navigate to area from search result, delete an area. Should run in CI using a mock vision backend to avoid Ollama/Claude dependency.

## Frontend/browser bugs caught during manual testing (kitchinv-5mw)

These were found during a live mobile testing session and require a real browser to reproduce:

1. **Mobile file picker not opening**: The original design hid the file input with `position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px` inside a `<label>` wrapper, relying on implicit label/input association. On mobile browsers this silently failed — tapping the label did not open the file picker. Fixed by replacing with a plain visible `input[type="file"]` and removing the global `display: none` CSS that was hiding it. An E2E test in mobile viewport (e.g. Playwright's iPhone emulation) should tap the "Choose photo" element and assert the file chooser is triggered.

2. **capture="environment" forcing camera**: The `capture` attribute bypassed the camera/library sheet on iOS, confusing users who wanted to upload an existing photo. Removed. E2E test: assert no `capture` attribute on the file input.

3. **Context cancellation killing Ollama on navigation**: When the user navigated away mid-stream, the browser cancelled the fetch, which cancelled the HTTP request context, which killed the Ollama API call. Fixed by using `context.WithoutCancel` for the vision analysis call so it runs to completion regardless of client disconnect. E2E test: start upload, navigate away immediately, return — assert items eventually appear (requires slow mock vision backend).

4. **sessionStorage unreliable on mobile Safari**: The original navigation-resilience approach wrote a key to sessionStorage on stream start and read it on page load to resume the scanning state. Mobile Safari (and bfcache behaviour) made this unreliable — the key was never read back. Fixed by removing sessionStorage entirely: the server-rendered template now exposes whether a photo exists with no items, and the page polls /areas/{id}/items automatically in that state. E2E test: assert that returning to an area page mid-analysis shows the scanning spinner without any client-side state.

5. **Areas list page not reflecting analysis state**: The area card on the main /areas page showed "No items" even when analysis was in progress. Fixed by adding a third template branch: photo present + no items → show "Analysing…" in accent colour. E2E test: after starting analysis and navigating to /areas, assert the card shows "Analysing" rather than the no-items message.

## Tool recommendation

Node v22 is already installed. Options:
- Playwright: real browser (Chromium/Firefox/WebKit), can test file input, fetch/ReadableStream, navigation, and mobile viewport emulation. Covers all bugs above.
- Vitest + jsdom: unit-tests extracted JS functions, fast, no browser. Cannot reproduce any of the mobile-specific bugs above.
- Playwright is clearly the right choice given the mobile-specific nature of the bugs found.

## Key flows to cover

- Add an area
- Upload a photo (file picker opens, file selected, Analyse clicked)
- Verify items appear on the area detail page
- Navigate away mid-analysis, return, verify scanning spinner shown and items appear
- Navigate to /areas mid-analysis, verify card shows Analysing
- Search for an item
- Delete an area
notes:
# Plan: Add Playwright E2E Tests (kitchinv-dls)

## Context

kitchinv has Go integration tests covering server-side logic, but no browser-level tests. During mobile testing, several bugs were found that required a real browser to reproduce (file picker, HTMX navigation, SSE streaming, polling). The goal is a Playwright E2E suite that runs headless from SSH and in CI, using a mock Ollama server instead of a real vision model.

All tests are headless — no headed tests needed. Every bug in the issue is reproducible headless.

---

## Directory Structure

```
e2e/
  package.json
  package-lock.json          # committed; CI uses npm ci
  playwright.config.ts
  global-setup.ts
  global-teardown.ts
  mock-ollama.js             # Node.js HTTP server faking Ollama API
  .gitignore                 # node_modules/, playwright-report/, test-results/
  tests/
    areas.spec.ts            # add, list, delete area
    upload.spec.ts           # upload photo, items stream in, button state
    navigation.spec.ts       # mid-analysis navigation bugs
    search.spec.ts           # search flow, navigate from result
    regression.spec.ts       # mobile file picker, no capture attr, no sessionStorage
```

---

## Mock Ollama Server (`e2e/mock-ollama.js`)

The Ollama adapter (`internal/vision/ollama/ollama.go`) POSTs to `/api/generate` with `stream: true` and reads NDJSON. Each line: `{"response":"<token>","done":false}`. The adapter accumulates tokens until it sees `\n`, then parses the line as `name | qty | notes`.

Confirmed from `ollama_test.go`:
```js
// Pattern the mock must replicate exactly:
{"response": "Milk | 1 liter | opened", "done": false}
{"response": "\n", "done": false}
{"response": "Butter | 1 block |", "done": false}
{"response": "\n", "done": true}
```

Mock items (fast mode, returned immediately):
- `Milk | 2 liters | semi-skimmed`
- `Butter | 1 block | opened`
- `Orange Juice | 1 carton |`

**Slow mode**: triggered by `X-Mock-Slow: true` request header. Delays 500ms between items. Used for navigation/mid-analysis tests via Playwright `page.route()` header injection.

Mock also handles:
- `GET /` → 200 (health check for `waitForPort`)
- `POST /api/generate` with `stream: false` → single JSON response (for non-streaming path)
- `Content-Type: application/x-ndjson` on streaming response; must flush each line with `res.write()`

---

## Global Setup (`e2e/global-setup.ts`)

1. `execSync('make build', { cwd: ROOT })` — builds `./kitchinv` binary (Makefile handles Go path discovery)
2. `fs.mkdtempSync(...)` — creates temp dir for SQLite DB and photos
3. Spawn `node e2e/mock-ollama.js` with `MOCK_OLLAMA_PORT=19434`
4. Spawn `./kitchinv` with env:
   ```
   LISTEN_ADDR=:9090
   DB_PATH=<tmpdir>/kitchinv.db
   PHOTO_LOCAL_PATH=<tmpdir>/photos
   VISION_BACKEND=ollama
   OLLAMA_HOST=http://localhost:19434
   OLLAMA_MODEL=moondream
   ```
5. `waitForPort(19434)` then `waitForPort(9090)` — polls HTTP until 200/redirect received
6. Write PIDs and tmpdir path to `/tmp/kitchinv-e2e-*.pid` files for teardown

**`waitForPort`**: retries `http.get` every 200ms, rejects after 15s. Any HTTP response (including 303 redirect from `/` → `/areas`) counts as ready.

## Global Teardown (`e2e/global-teardown.ts`)

Reads PID files, sends `SIGTERM`, deletes temp dir with `fs.rmSync(..., { recursive: true })`.

---

## `e2e/playwright.config.ts`

```
baseURL: http://localhost:9090
headless: true
timeout: 30_000
actionTimeout: 10_000
retries: 0
workers: CI ? 2 : undefined   (limits SQLite contention in CI)
reporter: CI ? 'github' : 'list'
trace: 'on-first-retry'
screenshot: 'only-on-failure'

projects:
  - chromium (Desktop Chrome) — used by all spec files
  - mobile-chrome (iPhone 14) — used by regression.spec.ts mobile test via test.use()
```

---

## Test Isolation Strategy

All tests share one running server + SQLite DB. Each test creates its own uniquely named area and filters all assertions by that name. No shared state, no cleanup needed between tests.

Pattern used everywhere:
```ts
const name = 'E2E <TestName>';
// create area named `name`
// assert on locator('.area-row', { has: locator containing name })
```

---

## `e2e/package.json`

```json
{
  "name": "kitchinv-e2e",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "private": true,
  "scripts": { "test": "playwright test" },
  "devDependencies": { "@playwright/test": "^1.44.0" }
}
```

---

## Test File Summaries

### `tests/areas.spec.ts`
- Empty areas list on first load → `.empty-state` visible
- Add area → appears in `.area-row-name a`
- Add area → click link → `.detail-title` matches name
- Delete area → `page.on('dialog', d => d.accept())` → redirects to `/areas`, card gone
- Two areas → `.area-row` count = 2

**Key**: `createArea(page, name)` helper: `goto('/areas')` → `click('#menu-btn')` → `fill('input[name="name"]')` → `click('button[type="submit"]')` → wait for `.area-row-name a` with that text. Must always start from `/areas` because HTMX target `.area-list` only exists there.

### `tests/upload.spec.ts`
- Upload triggers file chooser: `Promise.all([page.waitForEvent('filechooser'), page.click('#photo-input')])`
- Scanning indicator `.analyse-scanning` appears immediately after submit
- 3 `.item-row` elements appear after stream completes (fast mock)
- Button `#upload-btn` is disabled during upload, re-enabled after
- Second upload replaces items (still 3 `.item-row` after)

**JPEG fixture**: 512-byte buffer with `0xFF 0xD8 0xFF 0xE0` magic bytes. Written to tmp file via `fs.writeFileSync`. Server validates magic bytes — zero-byte file returns 400.

### `tests/navigation.spec.ts`

All tests inject slow header before upload:
```ts
await page.route('**/photos/stream', async route => {
  const headers = { ...route.request().headers(), 'x-mock-slow': 'true' };
  await route.continue({ headers });
});
```

**Test 1 — navigate away mid-stream, return, spinner shows then items appear:**
1. Upload with slow mock
2. Wait for `.analyse-scanning` (stream started)
3. `goto('/areas')` — disconnects browser from SSE stream
4. Click back to area detail
5. Server-rendered page: photo exists, no items → JS sets `.analyse-scanning`, starts polling
6. `waitFor('.item-row', count: 3, timeout: 20_000)` — polling picks up items after analysis completes server-side

**Test 2 — "Analysing..." card on /areas during analysis:**
1. Upload with slow mock
2. Wait for `.analyse-scanning`
3. `goto('/areas')`
4. Assert `card.locator('.area-row-analysing')` visible within 5s
5. Assert `.area-row-no-items` not visible

**Test 3 — items appear after navigation (context.WithoutCancel):**
1. Upload with slow mock, navigate away immediately
2. `waitForTimeout(2000)` — let slow mock finish (3 items × 500ms = 1.5s)
3. Navigate back, assert 3 `.item-row` elements (items came from DB, not SSE stream)

### `tests/search.spec.ts`

Helper `createAreaWithItems(page, name)`: create area + upload fixture + wait for 3 `.item-row`.

- Search 'Milk' → `.result-card` with 'Milk' visible within 5s
- Search result has `.result-area-link` ('View area')
- Click 'View area' → URL matches `/areas/\d+`, `.detail-title` contains area name
- Search unknown term → `.empty-state` visible
- Search lowercase 'milk' finds 'Milk' (case-insensitive DB query)

### `tests/regression.spec.ts`

**Bug 1 — No `capture` attribute:**
```ts
const attr = await page.locator('#photo-input').getAttribute('capture');
expect(attr).toBeNull();
```

**Bug 2 — Mobile file picker opens:**
```ts
test.use({ ...devices['iPhone 14'] });
const [fc] = await Promise.all([
  page.waitForEvent('filechooser', { timeout: 5_000 }),
  page.locator('#photo-input').tap(),
]);
expect(fc).toBeTruthy();
```

**Bug 3 — No sessionStorage (server-rendered spinner):**
1. Start upload with slow mock, wait for `.analyse-scanning`
2. Get current URL
3. `goto('/areas')`
4. Open fresh page in same context: `page.context().newPage()`
5. Fresh page navigates to area URL
6. Assert `.analyse-scanning` visible (server-rendered: `hasPhoto && !hasItems`)
7. Assert `Object.keys(sessionStorage).length === 0`

**Bug 4 — "Analysing..." not "No items" on areas list:**
Same as navigation.spec.ts Test 2, but as an explicit regression assertion with both positive (`.area-row-analysing` visible) and negative (`.area-row-no-items` not visible) assertions.

---

## Makefile Addition

```makefile
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# E2E tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
e2e:
	cd e2e && npm install --prefer-offline && npx playwright install --with-deps chromium && npx playwright test

e2e-headed:
	cd e2e && npm install --prefer-offline && npx playwright test --headed
```

Add `e2e` to `.PHONY`.

---

## CI Addition (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`)

New job, parallel to existing five:

```yaml
e2e:
  name: E2E Tests
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - uses: actions/setup-go@v5
      with:
        go-version-file: go.mod
        cache: true
    - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
      with:
        node-version: '22'
        cache: 'npm'
        cache-dependency-path: e2e/package-lock.json
    - name: Install Playwright
      run: cd e2e && npm ci && npx playwright install --with-deps chromium
    - name: Run E2E tests
      run: cd e2e && npx playwright test
      env:
        APP_PORT: '9090'
        OLLAMA_PORT: '19434'
        CI: 'true'
    - name: Upload report
      if: always()
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      with:
        name: playwright-report
        path: e2e/playwright-report/
        retention-days: 7
    - name: Upload traces
      if: failure()
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      with:
        name: playwright-traces
        path: e2e/test-results/
        retention-days: 7
```

**Note**: `npx playwright install --with-deps chromium` installs Chromium system dependencies via apt on ubuntu-latest (requires sudo, available on GH runners).

---

## Gotchas

1. **`make build` in global-setup**: The Makefile uses `$(GO)` which auto-discovers Go path. Ensure the CI `setup-go` step runs before the E2E job, or that Go is on PATH in `global-setup.ts`.

2. **HTMX `hx-target=".area-list"`**: The "Add area" form only works correctly when `.area-list` exists in the DOM (i.e., on `/areas` page). `createArea` must always `goto('/areas')` first.

3. **2-second polling delay**: The polling JS waits 2s before first poll. Navigation tests that depend on polling need `timeout: 20_000`.

4. **`page.route()` and fetch interception**: Playwright intercepts `fetch()` calls from the page (not just navigations). `route.continue({ headers })` correctly merges the `X-Mock-Slow` header.

5. **`hx-confirm` dialog**: HTMX uses native `window.confirm`. Register `page.on('dialog', d => d.accept())` before clicking delete.

6. **`package-lock.json` must be committed**: CI uses `npm ci`.

7. **Port choices**: 9090 (app) and 19434 (mock Ollama) are uncommon enough to avoid CI conflicts.

---

## Critical Files

- `internal/vision/ollama/ollama.go` — confirms NDJSON streaming format for mock
- `internal/vision/ollama/ollama_test.go` — confirms exact chunk pattern
- `internal/web/templates/pages/area_detail.html` — all key selectors + JS logic
- `internal/web/templates/partials/area_card.html` — `.area-row-analysing` template branch
- `internal/web/templates/base.html` — dropdown form structure, HTMX targets
- `internal/web/templates/partials/search_results.html` — `.result-card`, `.result-area-link`
- `Makefile` — binary output location (`-o kitchinv`), Go tool discovery pattern
close_reason: Implemented: 26 Playwright E2E tests merged via PR #11

title: Domain types and store layer
description: Create internal/domain/types.go with Area, Photo, Item structs. Implement internal/store/area_store.go, item_store.go, photo_store.go as thin wrappers around database/sql. item_store must include a Search method using LIKE on items.name COLLATE NOCASE. Write store tests using in-memory SQLite.

title: Remove item streaming: replace with single update after analysis completes
description: In the frontend redesign, items no longer stream into the table during analysis — the streaming behaviour is broken/invisible in the new card-based UI. Rather than fixing the streaming UX, remove the streaming code and replace it with a single atomic update that populates the item list when analysis fully completes. This simplifies the codebase and aligns with the new design where items appear in a card after upload.
close_reason: Removed all SSE streaming. Frontend now uses plain POST. PR #32 merged.

title: Write Unraid Community Apps template
description: Create an Unraid Community Apps XML template for kitchinv. The template should: pull from GHCR (ghcr.io/vbonduro/kitchinv), map /data for DB and photos, expose port 8080, configure GEMINI_API_KEY_FILE pointing to /data/secrets/gemini_key, and include a WebUI link. Store template in deploy/unraid/kitchinv.xml.

title: HTTP handlers and server wiring
description: Create internal/web/server.go (ServeMux routing + logging/recovery middleware). Create handler_area.go (list, create, detail, delete area), handler_upload.go (POST /areas/{id}/photos multipart upload), handler_search.go (GET /search with q param). All handlers detect HX-Request: true header and return partial vs full page accordingly. Routes: GET /areas, POST /areas, GET /areas/{id}, POST /areas/{id}/photos, GET /areas/{id}/photo, DELETE /areas/{id}, GET /search. Write handler tests using httptest.NewRecorder + mock service.

title: Search returns items from deleted areas
description: When an area is deleted, its items still appear in search results. The search query likely doesn't filter out items whose parent area has been deleted. Need to either cascade-delete items when an area is deleted, or filter them out in the search query.
close_reason: Fixed: INNER JOIN areas in search SQL; PR #26

title: Document update flow for Unraid deployment
description: Document how to update kitchinv on Unraid when a new version is released. Since this is a home server there is no auto-deploy. The flow is: new semver tag pushed → GHCR publish workflow builds and pushes image → user pulls new image in Unraid UI (or via docker pull) and restarts the container. Document this in README and consider whether a Watchtower container (auto-pulls updated images) is appropriate to recommend.

title: Add vision system status monitor to UI
description: Add a status indicator to the website showing whether the vision backend (Ollama/Claude) is currently reachable and ready. This involves three phases: (1) design the frontend indicator in the banner, (2) design the health-check API endpoint, (3) implement both end-to-end.

title: Improve gemini-2.5-flash item identification accuracy
description:
Current adjusted accuracy is ~54% on 6 fixtures. Goal is to improve the quality of gemini-2.5-flash's raw detections — better item names, more complete enumeration — not scorer tuning.

## Approaches to investigate

### 1. Prompt engineering via RALPH loop
Use the RALPH (Recursive Autonomous LLM Prompt Heuristics) loop to iteratively refine the vision prompt using benchmark fixture results as the eval signal. See: https://claude.com/plugins/ralph-loop

The loop: run benchmark → inspect misses → generate improved prompt → re-run → compare score → repeat until gains plateau.

### 2. Gemini File Upload API
For benchmarking we repeatedly submit the same 6 fixture images. Use the Gemini File Upload API instead of inline base64 to upload each image once and reuse the file URI across prompt iterations. This will be faster and cheaper for iterative prompt tuning runs.

See: https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/image-understanding

### 3. Known model weaknesses to target via prompt
Based on benchmark analysis:
- Dense pantry shelves: model detects ~40/51 items but names at category level
- Freezer: describes packaging instead of inferring the item (e.g. 'Frozen white-wrapped items' instead of 'bread')
- Fridge door condiments: sriracha, tamari, soy sauce, maple syrup frequently missed
- Items not directly visible or obscured consistently missed

## Success metric
Improve gemini-2.5-flash adjusted item accuracy from ~54% to >65% on the 6 benchmark fixtures.
close_reason: Gemini-tuned prompt + expanded overrides shipped. Avg item accuracy improved from ~54% to ~62% across 5 runs, touching 65% on best run. Two-pass self-review investigated and documented but not shipped due to high variance.

title: Allow multiple simultaneous photo uploads/analysis
description: Currently only one photo upload/analysis can be in progress per area at a time. The UX should allow a user to trigger uploads on multiple areas simultaneously without blocking or interfering with each other. This likely involves ensuring the backend handles concurrent uploads correctly (no DB race conditions, proper locking per area) and the frontend doesn't block the UI during analysis of one area when another is in progress.

title: Review unit tests for behaviour-based coverage
description: Audit all existing unit tests to ensure they test observable behaviour rather than implementation details. Identify tests that are brittle (testing internal state, private methods, or exact call sequences) and refactor them to test outcomes. Also identify any gaps where behaviour is untested. Deliverable: refactored tests + a short summary of what was changed and what gaps remain.
close_reason: Tests refactored and coverage gaps filled. PR #4 merged.

title: Allow users to reorder areas by drag and drop
description: Users should be able to click and drag an area card (by its title) to reorder areas. The order should persist across page reloads.

title: Add *_KEY_FILE support to config.go
description: Add GEMINI_API_KEY_FILE and CLAUDE_API_KEY_FILE environment variable support to internal/config/config.go. If the *_KEY_FILE var is set, read the key from that file path instead of from the *_KEY env var directly. This prevents API keys from appearing in process listings (ps auxe), docker inspect output, and /proc/<pid>/environ. The file should be read at startup and the key stored in memory. Return a clear error if the file is unreadable.

title: Production feedback loop for vision accuracy
description:
We have a benchmark scoring system but no way to measure prompt effectiveness against real production photos. We need a mechanism to collect ground truth from users and score the model's output against it, enabling continuous prompt improvement without relying solely on the 6 benchmark fixtures.

## Problem

The benchmark fixtures are static — they don't represent the full diversity of real kitchens, lighting conditions, or storage styles. Prompt improvements that score well on benchmarks may not generalise, and regressions in production would go undetected.

## Approaches to investigate

### 1. User correction as implicit signal
When a user edits or deletes an item after analysis, that's a signal the model got it wrong. Track edits/deletions in the DB and surface them as accuracy signal.

### 2. Explicit thumbs up/down per analysis
After an upload completes, show a simple 'Was this accurate?' prompt. Store the response alongside the photo and model output.

### 3. Export real photos + model output as new benchmark fixtures
Allow the server (in a dev/test mode) to save incoming photos + model JSON to disk in benchmark fixture format. Run the benchmark scorer offline against user-corrected ground truth.

### 4. Scorer dashboard
Aggregate the production signals into a dashboard or CLI report showing accuracy trends over time and per prompt version.

## Success metric
Ability to detect a >5% accuracy regression in production within a week of a prompt change, without requiring manual fixture creation.

title: Areas: add area button disappears after first area is created
description: After creating the first area, the 'add area' button does not appear in the UI. The user has to manually refresh the page to see it. The button should remain visible (or re-appear) after a new area is successfully created, without requiring a page refresh.

title: Add Docker publish workflow for container registry
description: Create a GitHub Actions workflow to build and publish the Docker container to a container registry (GitHub Container Registry / ghcr.io) on push to main or on tag/release. Should use the existing Dockerfile and multi-stage build.
close_reason: Created .github/workflows/docker-publish.yml publishing to ghcr.io on push to main and version tags

title: Use pre-commit to manage git hooks
description: Currently the gitleaks pre-commit hook is installed manually into .git/hooks/pre-commit, which means new contributors (and new worktrees) have to set it up themselves. Switch to pre-commit (https://pre-commit.com) to manage hooks declaratively via a committed .pre-commit-config.yaml. This makes hook installation a single command ('pre-commit install') and ensures everyone on the project runs the same checks. The gitleaks hook should be migrated to use the official gitleaks pre-commit hook (https://github.com/gitleaks/gitleaks). Consider also adding other useful hooks like trailing-whitespace, end-of-file-fixer, and check-yaml from pre-commit-hooks.

title: Wire cmd/kitchinv/main.go
description: Complete cmd/kitchinv/main.go with full dependency injection: load config, open DB, create stores, select vision adapter based on VISION_BACKEND env var (ollama default, claude alternative), select photo store based on PHOTO_BACKEND env var, construct AreaService, construct and start HTTP server. Smoke test: run locally with VISION_BACKEND=ollama, open browser, verify UI works end to end.

title: Update photo display immediately on upload (before analysis completes)
description: Currently the photo only appears after a full round-trip. As soon as the user selects a file and hits Analyse, the photo should update in the UI immediately using a local object URL (URL.createObjectURL), before the server responds. This improves perceived responsiveness.

title: Add integration tests
description: Write integration tests that exercise multiple layers together (e.g. HTTP handler + service + real SQLite DB). Use httptest.Server to test full request/response cycles for key flows: create area, upload photo, list items, search, delete area. Tests should run in CI without external dependencies.
notes: IMPORTANT: The stream endpoint integration test must include a case that sends a real multipart/form-data POST with a non-empty JPEG (or similar image file) to POST /areas/{id}/photos/stream and asserts that the vision adapter receives non-zero image bytes. This catches the regression where FormData was constructed after fileInput was disabled — causing the browser to silently exclude the disabled input, so the server received an empty image. The test should use httptest.Server + a stub StreamAnalyzer that records the image bytes it was called with, and assert len(imageData) > 0.
close_reason: Integration tests implemented and merged via PR #8

title: Add code coverage reporting with Codecov on CI
description: Integrate code coverage reporting into the CI pipeline. Run tests with coverage on every PR, upload results to Codecov, and add a coverage badge to the README. Configure minimum coverage thresholds to fail PRs that drop coverage.

title: Search: does not filter areas or items as you type
description:
The search function does not work correctly. Expected behavior: as the user types in the search box, only areas containing matching items should be shown, and within those areas only the matching items should be visible. The list should update in real-time as the user types (no submit required). Currently none of this filtering happens.

Additional requirements:
- Areas with no matching items should be hidden entirely
- If matching items are in a collapsed area (items hidden behind the toggle), the area should expand to show them
- Filtering should happen client-side for instant feedback as the user types
close_reason: Fixed: per-row item filtering in search; PR #25

title: Area image: replace and delete icons always visible instead of hover/press only
description: The replace image button and delete (x) icon on area photos are always visible. They should only appear on hover (desktop) or long-press/touch-hold (mobile). When not hovering or pressing, the photo should be shown cleanly without any overlaid controls.
close_reason: Fixed: hover-only on desktop, tap-to-reveal on mobile; PR #27

title: Frontend rework: single-page light-themed design with CRUD
description: Replace multi-page dark frontend with single-page /areas showing all area cards inline. Add backend CRUD endpoints (rename area, manage items, remove photo), rewrite CSS to light theme, rewrite E2E tests.
close_reason: Completed and merged in feat-frontend-rework PR

title: Fix flaky E2E tests: race between client spinner and server photo commit
description: Tests that check .analyse-scanning before navigating away have a race condition: the spinner appears immediately in JS (before the server even receives the upload), but the test navigates away expecting the photo to already be in the DB. Fix: wait for the /photos/stream response to start before navigating, since the server only responds after committing the photo.

title: Claude vision adapter
description: Implement internal/vision/claude/claude.go as ClaudeAnalyzer implementing VisionAnalyzer. Uses github.com/liushuangls/go-anthropic/v2. Sends image as base64 content block. Same structured prompt as Ollama adapter, same parseResponse helper. Write claude_test.go using httptest.NewServer with canned Anthropic API JSON responses.

title: Display inventory list as a table per area
description: Change the inventory list view for each area from a plain list into a table format. The table should display relevant item fields (name, quantity, unit, etc.) in a structured, scannable layout.

title: HTML templates
description: Create all templates in internal/web/templates/. Structure: base.html (full page layout with Pico.css CDN + HTMX CDN), partials/area_card.html, partials/area_list.html, partials/item_list.html, partials/search_results.html, pages/areas.html, pages/area_detail.html, pages/search.html. Create embed.go with //go:embed directive. Key mobile feature: file input must use accept='image/*' capture='environment' to open rear camera. Write a test verifying templates parse without error.

title: Add CI pipeline: lint, static analysis, code coverage on push
description: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on every push and PR. Should run: golangci-lint (configured via .golangci.yml), go vet, staticcheck, and go test with -race -coverprofile to compute code coverage.
close_reason: Created .github/workflows/ci.yml with lint, vet, staticcheck, and test+coverage jobs

title: Upload failure leaves area stuck in 'analysing' state permanently
description:
When a photo upload fails during analysis (e.g. image too large for Claude API), the area is left in a broken state: the photo is saved but analysis never completes. On page refresh, the area shows the 'analysing' banner indefinitely and the upload button is inaccessible, with no way to recover without manual DB intervention.

Root cause from logs (2026-02-28T07:47:05):
  POST /areas/10/photos/stream returned HTTP 500
  Error: 'failed to start vision stream: claude returned status 400: image exceeds 5 MB maximum: 8161424 bytes > 5242880 bytes'
  The photo was already persisted to the area before the Claude API call failed.

Expected behaviour:
- On analysis failure, the area photo should be rolled back / cleared so the user can retry
- Or at minimum the UI should allow re-uploading to an area stuck in a failed state

Related: kitchinv-ywq (upload banner states), kitchinv-85h (SSE auto-update)

title: Add E2E tests to CI pipeline
description:
Run Playwright E2E tests in CI against a containerised kitchinv instance, using a mock vision backend so there is no Ollama/Claude dependency.

The existing Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml provide a good starting point. The CI job should:
- Build the Docker image
- Start kitchinv with VISION_BACKEND=stub (or similar mock) and an in-memory/temp DB
- Run Playwright tests against it
- Tear down on completion

This job can run on every PR alongside the existing lint/test jobs, or only on merge to main to keep PR feedback fast. Depends on kitchinv-dls (E2E test suite) existing first.

See kitchinv-dls for Playwright vs Vitest tradeoff notes and specific bugs (photo preview onchange, mid-stream navigation) that require a real browser to catch.

title: Design: item preservation policy when re-uploading a photo
description:
When a user uploads a new photo to an area, the current behaviour is to replace all items entirely with AI-detected results. We need to decide the correct behaviour for the following cases:

1. No image uploaded yet, user has manually added items, then uploads a photo — should AI results replace the manual items, merge with them, or prompt the user?

2. Image already uploaded, user has manually added or edited items, then uploads a new image — should AI results replace everything (including user edits), merge, or prompt?

3. Upload fails — the existing items (including user-added/edited) should be preserved. This is now fixed in the UI but the policy for cases 1 and 2 is still unresolved.

Also needs consideration: should edited items (where the user changed an AI-detected item) be treated differently from user-added items when a re-upload occurs?

This is a design decision before implementation.

title: Design and implement edit mode vs view mode
description: Introduce a modal UX concept: view mode (default, read-only browsing) and edit mode (mutations: add/delete areas, reorder, edit items). A pencil button in the top banner toggles edit mode. Define exactly what is available in each mode, document it in a user guide, and add a help menu (? button in top banner) that explains the app. The help menu and user guide content should be defined as part of this issue.

title: Fix CI running twice (on push and PR)
description: CI checks are triggered twice — once for push events and once for pull_request events. When a PR is open, the push-triggered run is redundant. Fix the GitHub Actions workflow to only run once per PR/push, e.g. by limiting push triggers to main only and using pull_request for feature branches, or by using the 'push' event only when there is no associated PR.
close_reason: Fixed: push trigger limited to main, removed spurious playwright-report upload step. PR #12 merged.

title: Rework frontend based on Figma design
description: Replace the existing frontend with a new implementation based on the Figma design. Generated code is in ~/git/github.com/vbonduro/kitchinv/figma. This is a full UI overhaul.
close_reason: Completed and merged in feat-frontend-rework PR

title: VisionAnalyzer interface and parse helper
description: Create internal/vision/vision.go with VisionAnalyzer interface, AnalysisResult and DetectedItem types. Create internal/vision/parse.go with shared parseResponse(raw string) []DetectedItem helper that splits lines on '|' (name|quantity|notes format). Write table-driven parse_test.go covering edge cases: empty response, missing pipes, extra whitespace.

title: UX improvements for areas and uploads
description:
1. Remove 'Open' button from area cards on /areas page — make entire card clickable instead
2. Add delete button (garbage can icon) to top-right of each area card on /areas page and area detail page
3. Fix image clipping — ensure full image displays without cropping
4. Beautify file input — add camera icon next to 'Choose File' button and improve styling (may use Figma reference)

title: Fine-tune vision prompt and benchmark across models (Gemini, Sonnet, Opus, etc.)
description: Systematically evaluate and improve the vision analysis prompt quality by comparing results across multiple models. Goals: 1) Define a reproducible benchmark (set of reference images with known ground-truth item lists). 2) Run the same images through Claude Sonnet, Opus, Gemini (via API), and local Ollama. 3) Score results on: item count accuracy, brand name specificity, quantity accuracy, location accuracy. 4) Iterate on system prompt and user prompt based on findings. 5) Consider making the model configurable at runtime (env var or config) to allow easy switching.
close_reason: Completed: benchmarked gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-3-flash-preview, claude-haiku/sonnet/opus across 6 fixtures. gemini-2.5-flash recommended as production backend at ~54% adjusted accuracy. Full results in docs/vision-model-benchmark.md.

title: Sticky area photo: anchor image while scrolling items, release when next area comes into view
description:
## Context

The user wants to refer to the area photo while reviewing the inventory list — e.g. to verify which item is which, or check quantities visually. The photo must stay in view while they scroll through the items.

## Behaviour

As the user scrolls down a long item list for an area card, the area photo should stick to the top of the viewport so it remains visible. When the next area card's photo scrolls into view, the sticky photo should release and scroll away naturally. On small item lists where no scroll is needed, nothing changes.

## Implementation notes

Likely uses IntersectionObserver to detect when a card's photo section enters/leaves the viewport, toggling a sticky class. CSS position:sticky scoped to each area card is another option. A sentinel element at the top of each card can signal when a new area is entering the viewport so the previously anchored photo releases.
close_reason: PR merged: sticky area photo + frozen thead + per-test E2E isolation

title: Upload: show 'Uploading...' in banner then transition to 'Analysing...'
description:
ORIGINAL INTENT: Show 'Uploading...' then transition to 'Analysing...' during photo upload.

DECISION: Simplified to a single 'Analyzing your space...' state shown immediately on file select. The two-phase transition was untestable on localhost (upload completes in <1ms) and added unnecessary complexity.

WHAT WAS DONE:
- JS overlay now shows 'Analyzing your space...' immediately on file select (not 'Uploading...' first)
- Removed the fetch .then() banner-switch code
- Fixed regression: server-rendered analysing overlay removed from template (area_card.html) — areas with photo+no-items no longer show a stuck overlay on page load
- Added remove button escape hatch (later removed in favour of full fix)
- Fixed stuck analysing state: page load with photo+no-items now shows photo with controls and empty items section
- Updated regression.spec.ts (Bug 3, Bug 4) and navigation.spec.ts to reflect new JS-only overlay behaviour

CURRENT ISSUE:
E2E test 'analyzing overlay appears immediately on file select' is failing. The test:
1. Closes the Ollama gate
2. Triggers file upload
3. Calls waitForGate (confirms stream is blocked)
4. Asserts analyzing-indicator overlay is visible

But the assertion fails with 'hidden'. The overlay IS injected by startStream JS synchronously on file select, but the snapshot shows no overlay — only the photo with replace/remove controls and items. This means finishStream() already ran and removed the overlay before or during waitForGate. Root cause unclear — possibly the gate is not holding the stream as expected with the current binary, or the overlay is being removed by finishStream before waitForGate returns.

BRANCH: feat-uploading-then-analysing
PR: #31 (open)
close_reason: Fixed: analyzing overlay was invisible because .area-photo-section collapsed to 0px when preview image had no alt text. Added min-height:120px to injected photo section. PR merged.

title: Fix linting CI check
description: The linting CI check is not currently working and needs to be fixed. Investigate the CI configuration and linting setup to identify the issue and resolve it so the lint check passes in CI.

title: Areas: duplicate add area buttons appear after deleting multiple areas
description: After deleting more than one area, two 'add area' buttons appear — one at the bottom of the screen and one in the middle. The expected behavior when all areas are deleted is a single centered 'add area' button (the empty state). A page refresh fixes it. The UI should correctly transition to the empty state with a single centered button after the last area is deleted.
close_reason: Fixed: remove btn-wrap on empty state transition; PR #28

title: Build vision benchmark runner
description: Create cmd/benchmark/ binary that takes a fixture directory (images + ground_truth.json with expected item lists), runs each image through a configured VisionAnalyzer, and scores results on: 1) item identification accuracy (primary) — did it find the right types of food items, 2) quantity accuracy (secondary) — were counts correct. Output JSON + human-readable summary. Should work with any VisionAnalyzer implementation (Claude Sonnet, Opus, Gemini).
